evals.prompt: Prüf mal meine Spring Boot Config auf Probleme
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive config audit with RED/YELLOW/GREEN verdict, unified severity table, cross-file analysis, timeout alignment diagram, and starter-conditional summary.
expectations: Finds spring.jpa.open-in-view defaulting to true as a WARNING or higher
Finds spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: update as CRITICAL or WARNING
Finds hardcoded spring.datasource.password as CRITICAL
Finds missing server.shutdown: graceful as WARNING or higher
Finds management.endpoints.web.exposure.include: * as CRITICAL
Finds spring.h2.console.enabled: true in base config as CRITICAL or WARNING
Finds spring.redis.host as deprecated property (should be spring.data.redis.host)
Finds logging.level.org.springframework.security: DEBUG in prod profile as CRITICAL or WARNING
Finds hardcoded password in application-prod.yml as CRITICAL
Finds spring.devtools in production profile as WARNING or higher
Finds HikariCP connectionTimeout vs Tomcat connection-timeout mismatch
Finds Feign timeout exceeding Resilience4j circuit breaker timeout
Finds Tomcat max-threads vs HikariCP pool size imbalance
Finds misspelled ddl_auto property (underscore vs hyphen)
Finds orphaned app.feature.toggles property without @ConfigurationProperties binding
Finds spring.flyway.enabled without Flyway dependency
Includes a RED/YELLOW/GREEN production readiness verdict
Output uses a single unified table with exactly CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO severity levels (not HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
Identifies cross-file override failure: ddl_auto underscore in prod may not override ddl-auto update in base
Includes a timeout alignment diagram or chain showing current vs recommended ordering
Includes starter-conditional analysis explaining which findings depend on which dependencies
Output ends with a summary and top 3 prioritized action items

evals.expected_output: A focused production config review with verdict, unified severity table, cross-file tracing, and timeout alignment.
expectations: Finds hardcoded spring.datasource.password in application-prod.yml as CRITICAL
Finds logging.level.org.springframework.security: DEBUG as CRITICAL or WARNING
Finds spring.devtools.restart.enabled: true in prod as WARNING or higher
Finds management.endpoint.env.show-values: ALWAYS as CRITICAL or WARNING
Finds HikariCP connectionTimeout (60s) vs Tomcat connection-timeout (10s) mismatch
Finds HikariCP maximum-pool-size: 5 as too small for Tomcat default threads
Finds misspelled ddl_auto property and explains it may not override the base config ddl-auto: update
Includes a RED/YELLOW/GREEN production readiness verdict
Output uses a single unified table with exactly CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO severity levels (not HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
Includes a timeout alignment diagram or chain showing current vs recommended ordering
Output ends with a summary and top 3 prioritized action items

evals.expected_output: A production-readiness assessment with clear RED verdict, unified table, cross-file analysis, and actionable top 3.
expectations: Clearly states the config is NOT production-ready with a RED verdict
Finds at least 3 CRITICAL severity issues
Finds hardcoded credentials in at least one config file
Finds unsecured actuator endpoints
Finds at least one timeout or pool size inconsistency
Finds at least one deprecated or misspelled property
Provides specific fix recommendations (not just identifies problems)
Output uses a single unified table with exactly CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO severity levels (not HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
Includes starter-conditional analysis explaining which findings depend on which dependencies
Output ends with a summary and top 3 prioritized action items
